# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a simple Dockerfile for our Go microservice. Compile the application and run the binary. Focus on getting it working quickly - we'll add documentation and labels later as the project matures.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Go runtime as parent image
FROM golang:1.16-alpine3.12

# Set the working directory in the container to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy go mod files into docker container
COPY go.* ./

# Run "go get" command to fetch all dependencies mentioned in go.mod file
RUN go get -d -v ./...

# Copy the source code from current directory to docker container's /app directory 
COPY . .

# Compile the Go app and produce binary output to /bin/app 
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -a -installsuffix cgo -o /usr/src/app

# Define default command when container starts
CMD [ "/usr/src/app" ]
